content:
9. Conclusion and Recommendations
Victory Goldfields tenements have been only sporadically explored in the past with companies exploring their own local tenements, with no
systematic exploration over the entire amalgamated package ever undertaken. In order to gain a clear picture of the effectiveness of the
historical exploration, all data has been compiled and placed into a regional geophysical and geological framework. Such a regional overview
has highlighted areas that are poorly or under explored, revealing new targets for further exploration, using modern day techniques.
 
There are numerous high-grade drill intercepts within M20/129, in the Oxonia Pit. RC drilling to test lateral and depth extensions is a high
priority. The mineralisation is open in all directions and has only been tested by shallow drilling programs.
 
Auger soil geochemistry, conducted by Fairbairn in 2020, suggest the mineralisation in the Oxonia Pit continues northwards into M20/360.
Statistics suggest that 20 ppb Au is anomalous in the data set. Assays up to 154 ppb Au make up the anomaly. The 50 ppb Au contour is
very robust and continuous along strike. Drill testing of this auger anomaly by RC holes on 200m spaced drill traverses is warranted. There is
no recorded historical drilling in the area.
 
Auger sampling has been successfully applied to the many old gold workings within the Antipodean, Warribrook, Maralinga Downs, Lady Nitouche,
Retreat and Saltire areas. Anomalous gold and arsenic geochemistry has been identified and targets will be prioritised for drilling.
 
Over 60 gold and copper mineral occurrences have been recorded within the Coe district and within Fairbairn Goldfield’s tenure, illustrated in
